title: Guard count_words against None and non-string input
description: The count_words(text) function is meant to return the number of whitespace-separated words in a string. It must return 0 when text is None or an empty string, and raise TypeError with a clear message when text is neither None nor a str. It currently calls text.split() unconditionally, so passing None raises AttributeError and passing other non-string types fails with confusing errors. Add the guards so the function returns 0 for None and empty strings, raises TypeError for non-string non-None inputs, and keeps the existing behaviour for normal strings.
